chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD Criminal Miscellaneous Application No.S-537 of 2019 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objections. 2. For hearing of main case. 18.04.2022 Mrs. Rehana Siddiqui, advocate for the applicant. Ms. Sana Memon, Assistant Prosecutor General, Sindh. == The facts in brief necessary for disposal of instant criminal miscellaneous application are that the applicant lodged an F.I.R Crime No.15 of 2019 with P.S Khokhrapar against the private respondents for offence punishable under sections 506, 147, 148, 149, 337-A(i) & 337-F(i) P.P.C, it was recommended by the police for its disposal under ‘NC’ Class and it was disposed of accordingly by learned Consumer Protection Court/Judicial Magistrate, Umerkot, vide order dated 08.10.2019, which is impugned by the applicant before this Court by way of instant Criminal Miscellaneous Application under section 561-A Cr.P.C. It is contended by learned counsel for the applicant that the learned trial Magistrate has passed the impugned order in slipshod manner, therefore, it is liable to be set-aside. Learned Assistant Prosecutor General, Sindh for the State by supporting the impugned order has sought for dismissal of instant criminal miscellaneous application. Heard arguments and perused the record. The FIR of the incident has been lodged with delay of about one month; such delay could not be overlooked. The injuries sustained by PW Murad were non-cognizable in nature. Parties are already disputed over landed property. On due investigation, the F.I.R of the incident was recommended by the police for its

Parties are already disputed over landed property. On due investigation, the F.I.R of the incident was recommended by the police for its disposal under ‘NC’ Class and it has rightly been disposed of accordingly by learned Trial Magistrate, by way of impugned order, which is not found to be illegal to be interfered with by this Court; consequently the instant criminal miscellaneous application is dismissed. JUDGE Muhammad Danish*
